title: 10 best herbal creams for hematuria

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Bearberry cream's astringent properties help to reduce the frequency and amount of urine excreted, which can alleviate symptoms of hematuria such as frequent urination.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Bearberry's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to hematuria, and protect against oxidative stress that may exacerbate the condition.

Urinary antiseptic properties: Bearberry cream's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi that may be present in the urine, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation that can contribute to hematuria.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arctostaphylos uva ursi, also known as bearberry, creams helps with hematuria because it contains arbutin and hydroquinone, compounds that have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of bearberry help to soothe and calm the bladder, reducing inflammation and infection. Additionally, bearberry's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, which may help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms associated with hematuria. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support urinary health.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating hematuria symptoms.
Arbutin: This phenolic glycoside has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat urinary tract infections (UTIs) that may be contributing to hematuria.

Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of hematuria by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of tannins and arbutin, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Fruits: They are utilized for their content of arbutin and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hematuria.
Barks: The bark of Arctostaphylos uva ursi is used due to its rich content of tannins and arbutin, which are thought to exhibit anti-inflammatory and astringent effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and berries in the summer months when they are in full bloom for 2 hours.
Dry the harvested leaves and berries in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried arctostaphylos uva ursi with 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Infuse the mixture for 4 hours then strain it to obtain the herbal extract for 4 hours.
Mix 5 grams of the herbal extract with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of shea butter and heat until melted for 10 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested roots at a low temperature of 50-60°c for 2-3 hours to preserve the medicinal properties.
Grind 100g of dried althaea officinalis root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Steep 10g of the ground althaea root powder in 100g of distilled water for 10-15 minutes to create a decoction.
Mix 5g of beeswax with 10g of the cooled decoction in a double boiler to create a thick herbal cream.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried solidago virgaurea flowers and 300ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil.

Add 50g of beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil to the infused oil mixture and heat gently.
Stir the mixture constantly until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture has cooled and thickened.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh portulaca oleracea leaves from a pesticide-free area at 8am.

Mix the blend with 20g of coconut oil and 5g of beeswax at 60c for 10 minutes.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, red clover roots help soothe and protect the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria.

Flowers: The flowers of red clover contain isoflavones, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract, making them an effective ingredient in creams for treating hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried trifolium pratense flowers in amounts of 20g per recipe prepare for processing.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle grind for 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered flowers with 50g of coconut oil and 25g of beeswax in a double boiler heat for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let steep for 2 hours allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the carrier oils.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve discarding solids pour into sterilized containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with hematuria because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically, silymarin can help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria. Additionally, silymarin's antioxidant activity helps protect the kidneys from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and regeneration of damaged tissue. By addressing these underlying causes, milk thistle creams can provide effective relief for individuals suffering from hematuria.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid extracted from milk thistle seeds, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of hematuria such as pain and discomfort.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle creams have been shown to have a protective effect on the kidneys and bladder, helping to prevent damage from oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hematuria.

Volatile oils: Volatile oils extracted from milk thistle seeds, such as silychristin, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the risk of infection and promote healing in cases of urinary tract inflammation associated with hematuria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the urinary tract.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds are used to create a soothing and protecting agent against inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried silybum marianum fruit and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered silybum marianum with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:10.
Add vitamin e oil and beeswax to the mixture, stirring until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Transfer the mixture to a double boiler and heat it for 30 minutes at low temperature.
Remove from heat, let cool, and whip the cream into a smooth consistency using an electric mixer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with hematuria because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the urinary tract. The cream's moisturizing properties also aid in reducing inflammation and irritation, which can cause pain and discomfort associated with hematuria. Additionally, elder cream's ability to promote healing and regeneration can help to accelerate the repair of damaged tissue in the bladder and urethra, ultimately reducing bleeding and improving overall symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elder cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and anthocyanins, which help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of hematuria, such as burning and pain.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in elder cream, including vitamins C and E, help protect the kidneys and bladder from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to hematuria.

Astringent: Elder cream's astringent properties, which come from its tannin content, help reduce bleeding and inflammation in the urinary tract by constricting blood vessels and drying up excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal irritated urinary tract tissues.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, ensuring at least 2 cups of plant material for the recipe.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Combine dried sambucus nigra with 1 cup of olive oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax, heating the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes before whipping the mixture into a smooth cream using an immersion blender for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to sterile glass jars and store in the refrigerator at 40°f or below for up to 6 months, allowing the cream to thicken over time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with hematuria because it contains the potent herb Achillea millefolium, which has been used for centuries to treat various urinary issues. The cream's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated tissues in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria. Additionally, yarrow's astringent properties can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, providing quick relief from heavy or painful urination.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Yarrow cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to hematuria (blood in urine).

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in yarrow cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of hematuria.

Haemostatic properties: Yarrow's haemostatic properties, including its ability to stimulate clotting factors and platelet aggregation, can help stop or prevent bleeding in the urinary tract, which is a major concern with hematuria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help alleviate hemorrhagic symptoms associated with hematuria.
Buds: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, these parts are used to create a soothing and calming effect on the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and pain caused by bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Prepare herbs for infusion by chopping leaves and flowers finely.
Infuse herbs in a carrier oil for at least 4 hours.
Strain the infusion and combine with beeswax and essential oils.
Whip the mixture to desired consistency for application as cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with hematuria because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate discomfort associated with bleeding. By soothing and calming the affected area, pot marigold creams provide relief from the burning sensation and pain often experienced during hematuria, allowing individuals to manage their condition more effectively and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hematuria.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of pot marigold cream's bioactive compounds, including carotenoids and polyphenols, helps neutralize free radicals that can damage kidney tissue and exacerbate bleeding.

Wound-healing and tissue-protective properties: Pot marigold cream's ability to promote wound healing and protect tissues from further damage may help reduce the severity of hematuria and prevent complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing infection associated with hematuria.

Barks: The barks of the pot marigold plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to urinary tract infections (UTIs), a common cause of hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly for use in the cream recipe.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the herb mixture in a heat-proof container for 6 to 8 weeks, shaking occasionally, to extract oils.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 1:2.
Whip the mixture until smooth and creamy, then pour into containers and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle creams help with hematuria because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and protect the urinary tract. The herbs in roselle, such as Hibiscus sabdariffa, have been traditionally used to treat various bladder issues, including inflammation and irritation. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, roselle creams may help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with hematuria, allowing for a more comfortable and normal urination process. This natural remedy has shown promise in reducing the severity and frequency of bleeding episodes in individuals affected by hematuria.
remedy_properties: Astringent and anti-inflammatory properties: These properties help reduce inflammation and shrink swollen blood vessels in the urinary tract, which can contribute to hematuria.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal Roselle creams contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative damage to the urinary tract lining, reducing the risk of bleeding and irritation.

Uroprotective and antispasmodic properties: These properties help soothe and calm the urinary tract, relaxing muscle spasms that can contribute to hematuria, and protecting the bladder and urethra from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, roots are effective in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of hematuria.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, rhyzomes exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for treating hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers measuring 20-30 grams and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered hibiscus sabdariffa with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant mixing bowl to create a decoction for 30 minutes.
Filter the decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids, retaining 50 milliliters of the extracted solution.
Mix the filtered solution with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes until the mixture reaches 65-70 degrees celsius.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at 4-6 degrees celsius to maintain potency and shelf life for up to two weeks.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hematuria are topical remedies that combine natural herbs with moisturizing properties to alleviate symptoms of hematuria, a condition characterized by blood in the urine. These creams typically contain soothing herbs such as aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Examples include creams with saw palmetto and stinging nettle, which have shown to improve urinary health and reduce symptoms of hematuria, allowing individuals to lead more comfortable and active lives.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hematuria that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations, rashes, and inflammation. For instance, eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis can be alleviated by topical applications of herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula. These natural remedies help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with these conditions. They may also aid in healing damaged skin tissues and promoting overall skin health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with hematuria is a blend of Plantain, Calendula, and Marshmallow root creams. Plantain cream soothes and calms the urinary tract, while Calendula cream promotes wound healing and reduces inflammation. Marshmallow root cream, rich in mucilage, forms a protective barrier and helps to reduce bleeding. Applied topically, these creams can help to alleviate symptoms and promote healing. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
